---
title: "History"
subtitle: "The knowledge-commit trail that built the graph"
toc: false
---

The formalization was assembled as a sequence of **Conventional Knowledge Commits**: each commit
that changes the mathematical state declares what it asserts, what it depends on, and what it
closes or breaks. Replaying them in order reconstructs the [claim graph](claimgraph.qmd) one step
at a time. The compact timeline below (a dependency-free SVG generated by
[`claimgraph`](https://github.com/hotherio/claimgraph)) shows each commit's effect on the nodes;
drag the slider through the history.

<iframe src="history-timeline.html" title="ClaimGraph history timeline" loading="lazy"
        style="width:100%;height:560px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:6px">
</iframe>

## Knowledge-commit log

The conventional commits that moved the mathematical state, oldest first. *Touches* lists the
claims each commit asserted, closed, or broke (its replay focus); plain chores and merges that
carry no knowledge footer do not appear.

{{< include _history-table.md >}}
